steps.title: Fastest path: note the given angles first
steps.content: List the given angle measures first: angle $M$ is $\frac{2\pi}{3}$ radians, and angle $P$ is $\frac{5\pi}{12}$ radians greater than angle $M$. This becomes a short addition and conversion problem once the givens are organized.

steps.title: Find P in radians by adding
steps.content:
Since P is $\frac{5\pi}{12}$ radians greater than M:
$$P = M + \frac{5\pi}{12} = \frac{2\pi}{3} + \frac{5\pi}{12}$$
Convert to common denominator 12:
$$\frac{2\pi}{3} = \frac{8\pi}{12}$$
So:
$$P = \frac{8\pi}{12} + \frac{5\pi}{12} = \frac{13\pi}{12}$$

steps.title: Convert to degrees
steps.content:
Multiply by the conversion factor $\frac{180^{\circ}}{\pi}$:
$$P = \frac{13\pi}{12} \cdot \frac{180^{\circ}}{\pi} = \frac{13 \cdot 180^{\circ}}{12} = \frac{2340^{\circ}}{12} = 195^{\circ}$$
That is the correct answer.
